audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for judging, measure, speck/log, holy/pearls, ask-seek-knock, golden rule, narrow gate, false prophets, fruit discernment, Lord/Lord warning, doing the Father's will, rock/sand houses, authority response.

verses.restored: Do not judge, so that you may not be judged.
For with the judgment by which you judge, you will be judged; and with the measure by which you measure, it will be measured to you.
Why do you look at the speck in your brother's or sister's eye, but do not notice the beam in your own eye?
Or how will you say to your brother or sister, Let me take the speck out of your eye, and look, the beam is in your own eye?
Hypocrite, first take the beam out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take the speck out of your brother's or sister's eye.
Do not give what is holy to dogs, and do not throw your pearls before pigs, lest they trample them with their feet and turn and tear you.
Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
For everyone who asks receives, the one who seeks finds, and to the one who knocks it will be opened.
Or what person among you, if his son asks for bread, will give him a stone?
Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a serpent?
If you then, being harmful/mixed, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in the heavens give good things to those who ask him?
Therefore all things, whatever you want people to do to you, so also do to them; for this is the Law and the Prophets.
Enter through the narrow gate, because wide is the gate and spacious is the way leading into destruction, and many are those entering through it.
How narrow the gate and compressed the way leading into life, and few are those finding it.
Pay attention to false prophets, who come to you in sheep's clothing, but inwardly are ravenous wolves.
From their fruits you will recognize them. Do people gather grapes from thorns, or figs from thistles?
In the same way, every good tree makes beautiful/good fruit, but the rotten tree makes harmful fruit.
A good tree is not able to make harmful fruit, nor a rotten tree to make beautiful/good fruit.
Every tree not making beautiful/good fruit is cut down and thrown into fire.
So then, from their fruits you will recognize them.
Not everyone saying to me, Lord, Lord, will enter the reign of the heavens, but the one doing the will of my Father in the heavens.
Many will say to me in that day, Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in your name, cast out unclean spirits in your name, and do many powers in your name?
Then I will confess to them: I never knew you. Depart from me, workers of lawlessness.
Therefore everyone who hears these words of mine and does them will be like a wise man who built his house on the rock.
The rain came down, the rivers came, and the winds blew and struck that house; but it did not fall, for it had been founded on the rock.
Everyone hearing these words of mine and not doing them will be like a foolish man who built his house on the sand.
The rain came down, the rivers came, and the winds blew and struck that house; and it fell, and its fall was great.
When Jesus finished these words, the crowds were astonished at his teaching.
For he was teaching them as one having authority, and not as their scribes.
